name: Agentic Workflow
principles: Each agent has one job, one output contract, and one handoff target.
No phase is complete until the next phase has enough evidence to continue without replaying the full chat.
Plans, code, tests, reviews, and memory updates are separate artifacts.
The workflow prefers small verified changes over broad unverified rewrites.
Durable memory records decisions and evidence, not private secrets or raw transcripts.

roles.mission: Convert an ambiguous request into a scoped implementation plan with acceptance criteria.
Make the smallest coherent code and documentation change that satisfies the plan.
Reproduce failures, isolate causes, and prove the implemented behavior works.
mustNot: Treat absence of an error as proof

roles.mission: Evaluate the change for correctness, maintainability, security, and fit with project patterns.
Preserve durable project learning after a completed workflow.

entryCriteria: A user goal or issue exists.
The plan names files or modules to inspect.
There is an implementation to verify.
exitCriteria: Relevant checks pass or failures are isolated.
Manual reproduction steps are documented when automated checks are insufficient.

entryCriteria: A diff and verification evidence exist.
exitCriteria: Findings are resolved or explicitly accepted.

entryCriteria: The change is complete or intentionally paused.
exitCriteria: Decision and outcome notes are stored.

qualityGates.description: The work has a bounded goal and explicit non-goals.
evidence: issue link, user request, or plan section

qualityGates.description: The agent inspected the relevant local code and current external docs when recency matters.
The implementation was checked with the strongest feasible local signal.
The next agent can continue from compact durable state.
